quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 11, Grade 11 Biology
quiz.subtitle: Cell Division: Mitosis Details, Chromosome Inheritance, and Spindle Function
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Chromosome Distribution, Zygote Chromosome Number, Telophase as Reverse of Prophase, Parental Resemblance, Centrioles in Animal Cells, Metaphase Alignment, Spindle Fiber Composition, Chromatid Separation Stages

questions.question: What ensures that each daughter cell receives an identical set of chromosomes?
options: Alignment and separation in metaphase and anaphase
Formation of spindle fibers in telophase
questions.explanation: The precise alignment of chromosomes at the metaphase plate and the equal separation of sister chromatids during anaphase ensure each daughter cell receives an identical set of chromosomes. DNA replication duplicates chromosomes but doesn't ensure equal distribution.

questions.question: If a human gamete contains 23 chromosomes, how many chromosomes does a zygote formed after fertilization have?
questions.explanation: A human gamete (sperm or egg) is haploid with 23 chromosomes. Fertilization combines two gametes, restoring the diploid number of 46 chromosomes in the zygote (23 from each parent).

questions.question: Which stage of mitosis is essentially the reverse of prophase?
questions.explanation: Telophase reverses the events of prophase: chromosomes decondense, nuclear envelope reforms, nucleolus reappears, and spindle disassembles. Prophase involves chromosome condensation, nuclear envelope breakdown, and spindle formation.

questions.question: Why do children resemble both parents?
options: They inherit 46 chromosomes from each parent
They inherit half their chromosomes from each parent
They inherit mitochondria from both parents
They undergo mitotic variation after birth
questions.explanation: Children inherit half their chromosomes (23) from their mother via the egg and half (23) from their father via the sperm. This combination of genetic material from both parents results in resemblance to both. Mitochondria are inherited only from the mother.

questions.question: Which of these structures, involved in cell division, is present in animal cells but not higher plant cells?
questions.explanation: Centrioles are present in animal cells but absent in higher plant cells. Plants organize spindle fibers using microtubule-organizing centers without centrioles. Centromeres, chromatids, and chromosomes are present in both plant and animal cells.

questions.question: Plants show mitotic divisions in
questions.explanation: Plants show mitotic divisions in both haploid and diploid cells. In the plant life cycle, mitosis occurs in haploid cells during gametophyte development and in diploid cells during sporophyte growth. This alternation of generations is unique to plants. For example, in angiosperms, the haploid pollen grain undergoes mitosis to form sperm cells, while diploid cells in roots and shoots undergo mitosis for growth.

questions.question: Division of nucleus without being followed by cytokinesis results into
questions.explanation: When nuclear division (mitosis or meiosis) occurs without subsequent cytokinesis, the result is a multinucleate cell containing multiple nuclei sharing the same cytoplasm. This is common in some fungi and certain specialized cells like skeletal muscle. Phragmoplast is a plant cell structure involved in cell plate formation. Polyploidy refers to having multiple sets of chromosomes. Uninucleate means one nucleus, which would not result from division without cytokinesis.

questions.question: A somatic cell that has just completed the S phase of its cell cycle, as compared to gamete of the same species, has:
options: same number of chromosomes but twice the amount of DNA
twice the number of chromosomes and four times the amount of DNA
four times the number of chromosomes and twice the amount of DNA
twice the number of chromosomes and twice the amount of DNA
questions.explanation: A somatic cell is diploid (2n), while a gamete is haploid (n). After S phase, DNA has replicated, so the somatic cell has 2n chromosomes but each chromosome consists of two sister chromatids, giving 4x the DNA content of a gamete. For example, if n=23, gamete has 23 chromosomes with 1x DNA, somatic cell after S phase has 46 chromosomes (2n) but with 4x DNA amount because each chromosome is duplicated.

questions.question: Spindle fibres are attached to
questions.explanation: Spindle fibers attach to the kinetochore, which is a protein structure assembled on the centromere. The kinetochore serves as the attachment site for microtubules and contains motor proteins that facilitate chromosome movement. Telomeres are the protective ends of chromosomes. Centromere is the region where kinetochores form. Kinetosomes are basal bodies associated with cilia and flagella, not chromosomes.

questions.question: The best stage to observe the shape, size and number of chromosomes is
questions.explanation: Metaphase is the best stage to observe chromosome shape, size, and number because chromosomes are fully condensed and aligned at the equatorial plate, making them clearly visible and distinguishable. In interphase, chromosomes are uncoiled as chromatin and not visible. In prophase, condensation is ongoing. In telophase, chromosomes begin decondensing.

questions.question: At which stage in mitosis and meiosis do chromosomes always line up at the equator of the spindle?
questions.explanation: During metaphase (metaphase I and II in meiosis, and metaphase in mitosis), chromosomes align at the equatorial plate (metaphase plate) with spindle fibers attached to kinetochores.

questions.question: Spindle fibres play an important role in mitosis and meiosis. Which biological molecule are spindle fibres composed of?
questions.explanation: Spindle fibers are composed of microtubules, which are polymers of tubulin protein. They are not made of polysaccharides (carbohydrates), nucleic acids (DNA/RNA), or lipids.

questions.question: During which of these stages of cell division do chromatids separate and move to the poles of the spindle?
options: Anaphase of mitosis and anaphase 1 of meiosis
Anaphase of mitosis and anaphase 2 of meiosis
questions.explanation: Sister chromatids separate during anaphase of mitosis and anaphase II of meiosis. Anaphase I separates homologous chromosomes, with sister chromatids remaining together.

questions.question: At which stage of mitosis do chromosomes uncoil and new nuclear envelopes form?
questions.explanation: During telophase, chromosomes decondense (uncoil) back into chromatin, and nuclear envelopes reform around each set of daughter chromosomes. This reverses the events of prophase.

questions.question: Vincristine is a chemotherapy drug used to treat cancer. It prevents spindle formation during mitosis. Which one of these events cannot therefore occur?
questions.explanation: If spindle formation is prevented, sister chromatids cannot separate during anaphase because there are no spindle fibers to pull them apart. DNA replication (S phase), chromosome coiling (prophase), and chromatid visibility occur independently of spindle formation.

scoreMessages.90: Outstanding! You've mastered all aspects of mitosis and meiosis! 🔬
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review chromosome behavior in different division stages.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on spindle fiber function and chromatid separation.
scoreMessages.0: Please review metaphase alignment, telophase events, and chromosome inheritance.
